creator: glarkin@FreeBSD.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

Aristedes Maniatis wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/133740; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Aristedes Maniatis <ari@ish.com.au>
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, jurgen@ish.com.au
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/133740: [new Port] www/archiva
> Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2009 12:13:30 +1000
> 
>  I don't think either javamail or Derby are hard dependencies. Although they are in the default config, a user might just as easily use MySQL as a database backend and not configure mail integration at all. So perhaps they should be considered optional extras that are up to the user to configure. Should this be handled with OPTIONS or with a message after installation?
>  
>  Ari Maniatis
>  
>  
>  -------------------------->
>  ish
>  http://www.ish.com.au
>  Level 1, 30 Wilson Street Newtown 2042 Australia
>  phone +61 2 9550 5001   fax +61 2 9550 4001
>  GPG fingerprint CBFB 84B4 738D 4E87 5E5C  5EFA EF6A 7D2E 3E49 102A

Hi Ari,

Thanks for getting back to me regarding the archiva port.  I found this
reference that indicates it requires javamail:

http://cwiki.apache.org/ARCHIVA/archiva-on-tomcat.html#ArchivaonTomcat-TheJavamail%2FActivationJARfiles

One of either Derby or MySQL has to be a dependency, as far as I can
tell.  In that case, can you add the MySQL JDBC database driver as the
dependency for now.  Maybe I'll get inspired to create a port for Derby
at some point.

If you do that, it would be helpful to update the archiva.xml file to
reference the MySQL driver, too.

Thank you,
Greg
- --
Greg Larkin
